documents.title: Solar Energy Basics
documents.text: Solar energy is power from the sun converted into electricity or heat using photovoltaic panels or solar thermal systems.

documents.title: Wind Energy Basics
documents.text: Wind energy uses turbines to convert the kinetic energy of moving air into electrical energy, often grouped in wind farms.

documents.title: Hydropower Basics
documents.text: Hydropower generates electricity by directing flowing water through turbines, typically at dams or run-of-river sites.

documents.title: Geothermal Energy Basics
documents.text: Geothermal energy taps heat from the Earth's interior to produce electricity or provide direct heating.

documents.title: Biomass Energy Basics
documents.text: Biomass energy comes from organic materials like wood or agricultural waste and can be burned or converted into fuels.
